name: nklisch
description: Reusable agent skills for software development workflows

skills.name: zod-v4
skills.description: Zod v4 validation library reference. Auto-loads for schemas, validation, z.object, safeParse, transforms.

skills.name: biome-v2
skills.description: Biome v2 linter/formatter reference. Auto-loads for biome config, linting rules, formatting.

skills.name: drizzle-v0
skills.description: Drizzle ORM v0.45+ PostgreSQL reference. Auto-loads for schema definitions, queries, migrations.

skills.name: hono-v4
skills.description: Hono v4 web framework reference. Auto-loads for routes, middleware, SSE, validation.

skills.name: tanstack-query-v5
skills.description: TanStack Query v5 (React Query) reference. Auto-loads for useQuery, useMutation, caching.

skills.name: tanstack-router-v1
skills.description: TanStack Router v1 reference. Auto-loads for file-based routing, loaders, search params.

skills.name: zustand-v5
skills.description: Zustand v5 state management reference. Auto-loads for stores, selectors, persist, middleware.

skills.name: tanstack-table-v8
skills.description: TanStack Table v8 headless table reference. Auto-loads for sorting, filtering, pagination, columns.

skills.name: clean-memory
skills.description: Audit, validate, and interactively refine MEMORY.md. Removes contradicted and redundant entries, trims bloat, then guides you section-by-section through what to keep.

skills.name: bun
skills.description: Bun runtime reference. Auto-loads for Bun.$, Bun.file, bun:test, shell commands, file I/O, and monorepo config.

skills.name: citty
skills.description: citty CLI framework reference (UnJS). Auto-loads for defineCommand, subcommands, argument definitions, lifecycle hooks.

skills.name: clack-prompts
skills.description: @clack/prompts terminal UI reference. Auto-loads for interactive CLI prompts, spinners, selects, confirms, and cancel handling.

skills.name: smol-toml
skills.description: smol-toml TOML parser/serializer reference. Auto-loads for parse, stringify, TOML config files.

skills.name: claude-cli-sdk
skills.description: @nklisch/claude-cli-sdk reference. Auto-loads when working with this SDK — spawns Claude CLI as a subprocess using Pro/Max billing (no API key needed).

skills.name: design-pages
skills.description: Design GitHub Pages for a project. Explores the codebase deeply, considers domain and audience, then pitches layout, content, structure, and visual identity choices.

skills.name: schemars
skills.description: schemars 1.x Rust JSON Schema generation reference. Auto-loads for JsonSchema derive, json_schema! macro, schema_for!, SchemaSettings, SchemaGenerator.

skills.name: claude-code-marketplace
skills.description: Research findings on Claude Code marketplace, plugin structure, and distribution. Auto-loads when working with marketplace.json, plugin.json, plugin distribution, Claude Code plugin format, SKILL.md frontmatter, tap.json integration, npm skill packages, agentskills.io standard, or designing how skilltap interacts with the Claude Code plugin ecosystem.

skills.name: codex-plugin-format
skills.description: OpenAI Codex CLI plugin, skill, and AGENTS.md format reference. Auto-loads when working with Codex plugins, .codex-plugin/plugin.json, Codex marketplaces, AGENTS.md, AGENTS.override.md, ~/.codex/config.toml, project_doc_fallback_filenames, agents/openai.yaml, the agentskills.io open standard, $plugin-creator, $skill-installer, codex plugin marketplace CLI, or designing dual Claude/Codex plugin distribution.

plugins.name: workflow
plugins.description: Software development workflow skills — ideate projects, design features, implement from plans, profile and optimize performance, refactor codebases with bold architectural reconceptions, extract patterns, design tests, fix bugs, review changes, and ship releases.

skills.name: autopilot
skills.description: Autonomously execute a project roadmap end-to-end. Loops design -> implement-orchestrator -> test for each phase, with judgment-driven refactoring passes. Tracks progress for cross-session resumption.

skills.name: bold-refactor
skills.description: Find beautiful code abstractions and cross-cutting simplifications. Applies conceptual lenses (elimination, unification, inversion) to surface bold architectural ideas, then converges into an implement-ready design document.

skills.name: cruft-cleaner
skills.description: Sweep a codebase for AI-accumulated cruft (dead code, stale comments, compatibility shims, defensive bloat, over-abstraction), triage findings by confidence tier, then orchestrate parallel cleanup agents with worktree isolation.

skills.name: design
skills.description: Create detailed DESIGN.md with implementation units. Use when translating a vision into code specs.

skills.name: e2e-test-design
skills.description: Design comprehensive e2e test suites with golden-path user journeys and adversarial failure-mode tests. Interactive — gathers failure expectations from the user.

skills.name: extend
skills.description: Add capability to an existing project — handles both small one-off features and major scope expansions. Reads foundation docs, explores the codebase, then branches based on scope: small additions produce a feature brief for design; large expansions update foundation docs and roadmap.

skills.name: extract-patterns
skills.description: Discover and document reusable code patterns. Use after implementation to capture conventions.

skills.name: fix
skills.description: Diagnose and repair a specific bug. Reproduces the issue, bisects to root cause, writes a failing test, applies the minimal fix, then confirms. Use when something is verifiably broken.

skills.name: ideate
skills.description: Interactive project definition workshop. Explores an idea through discovery, refinement, and requirements phases, then produces tailored foundation documents (VISION.md, SPEC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and domain-specific docs) based on the project's actual needs.

skills.name: implement
skills.description: Write code from a design or plan document. Use for designs targeting fewer than ~20 files or with tightly coupled units.

skills.name: implement-orchestrator
skills.description: Orchestrate implementation by spawning Sonnet task agents. Use for large designs (20+ files) or designs with independent subsystems that benefit from parallel implementation.

skills.name: perf-design
skills.description: Profile performance bottlenecks and design optimized solutions. Follows a strict optimization hierarchy: algorithmic fixes, I/O reduction, language idioms, then parallelism. Produces implementation-unit plans with benchmark scaffolds for implement or implement-orchestrator.

skills.name: principles
skills.description: Architectural and code-level principles (Ports & Adapters, Single Source of Truth, Generated Contracts, Fail Fast) plus Rolling-Foundation (foundation docs in docs/ describe vision and current intent — what is true now or imminent — never history; they roll forward in place). Auto-loads when designing, implementing, extending, or updating documentation.

skills.name: refactor-conventions-creator
skills.description: Create or update a project-specific refactor-conventions skill. Explores the repo, researches stack-specific best practices, interviews about both stylistic and structural preferences, then generates a combined skill with per-rule reference files.

skills.name: refactor-design
skills.description: Plan refactoring work. Use when code quality or structure needs improvement.

skills.name: release
skills.description: Prepare and publish a release. Drafts changelog entries from recent commits, confirms with user, then runs the release script.

skills.name: repo-eval
skills.description: Multi-dimensional codebase evaluation with verified scoring. Launches parallel explore agents, cross-checks findings, produces calibrated 1-10 scorecard across architecture, code quality, testing, documentation, CI/CD, error handling, security, DX, and maintainability with prioritized recommendations.

skills.name: research
skills.description: Research external libraries, APIs, SDKs, and patterns. Evaluates options against project needs, produces a research document and an auto-loading reference skill.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Review a specific code change — branch diff, commit, commit range, working tree, unpushed commits, or PR. Produces a prioritized review with Blockers, Important issues, and Nits. Different from /repo-eval and /security-review which audit the full repository.

skills.name: roadmap
skills.description: Generate an agent-optimized project roadmap from foundation documents. Reads VISION, SPEC, ARCHITECTURE, and other docs produced by /ideate, interviews about build style, then decomposes into chunky phases with dependencies, deliverables, and test checkpoints for AI-assisted development.

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Comprehensive security audit for any codebase. Discovers stack, lets user choose focus domains, researches best practices, produces scored markdown report with severity-classified findings.

skills.name: test-quality
skills.description: Improve test quality by deriving tests from specs, designs, and contracts — not from existing code. Use when finding gaps in test coverage, designing tests for interfaces and behavioral contracts, or systematically covering edge cases and branches from a specification perspective.

skills.name: tool-evaluator
skills.description: Structured self-evaluation of agent tool usage in the current conversation. Analyzes confusion points, inefficiencies, missing capabilities, and API surface friction. Produces a prioritized .md report with actionable recommendations for tool authors.

skills.name: update-documentation
skills.description: Align all documentation layers to code after implementing a feature. Covers internal specs, public website, LLM ingestion files, and project memory.

plugins.name: skill-authoring
plugins.description: Create, evaluate, and refine agent skills — write distributable reference skills for tools, refine skill ideas, and audit skill quality.

skills.name: skill-evaluator
skills.description: Evaluate agent skills against type-specific quality rubrics and emotional tone alignment. Classifies skills as reference, workflow, interactive, or principle, then scores against universal, type-specific, and emotional tone criteria. Produces scored evaluation reports with prioritized improvements, emotional tone rewrites, and test scenarios.

skills.name: skill-idea-refiner
skills.description: Interactively refine a rough skill idea into a well-designed agent skill. Guides through ideation, scoping, naming, description writing, structure decisions, and progressive disclosure, then scaffolds the skill files.

skills.name: write-tool-skill
skills.description: Write agent skills for external tools, CLIs, MCP servers, and libraries. Interactive workflow with research, structure decisions, and multi-skill breakout advice.

plugins.name: agile-workflow
plugins.description: Markdown-based work-tracking substrate for AI-driven projects. Sibling to workflow — items as files in .work/ with structured frontmatter, late-binding releases, gates that produce items, autopilot queue runner respecting depends_on graphs.

skills.name: principles
skills.description: agile-workflow principles — code-design (Ports & Adapters, SSOT, Generated Contracts, Fail Fast) and substrate-execution (Item-IS-the-Work, Rolling-Foundation, Late-Binding). Auto-loads during agile-workflow design/implement/review.

skills.name: ideate
skills.description: Interactive project definition workshop for agile-workflow. Discovery, refinement, and definitions phases produce foundation docs (VISION.md, SPEC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md) that encode the rolling-foundation principle. Use before /agile-workflow:convert.

skills.name: convert
skills.description: Bootstrap the agile-workflow substrate. Detects project shape (workflow-plugin / ad-hoc / no-tracking / greenfield), seeds initial items with per-design stage classification (drafting/implementing/review/done-shipped/done-archived), captures uncommitted in-flight working-tree work as substrate items via Explore-driven clustering, writes .claude/rules/agile-workflow.md, runs the conventions interview to populate .work/CONVENTIONS.md, copies work-view, appends CLAUDE.md section, produces MIGRATION_REPORT.md. Idempotent via --update.

skills.name: epicize
skills.description: Decompose foundation docs into multiple epics in .work/active/epics/ with declared depends_on chains. Reads VISION/SPEC/ARCHITECTURE, identifies capability arcs, proposes a decomposition, writes epic files at stage:drafting after user confirmation.

skills.name: park
skills.description: Quickly capture an idea into .work/backlog/ without disrupting current work. Triggers on 'park this', 'remind me about X', 'add to backlog'. Single-stride, low-friction capture; full sizing happens later via /agile-workflow:scope.

skills.name: scope
skills.description: Promote a backlog item or fresh request into .work/active/ as an epic, feature, or story with declared dependencies. For large scope (changes vision/spec/architecture), also rolls foundation docs forward in the same stride. Triggers on 'scope this', 'promote this', 'let's track this'.

skills.name: fix
skills.description: Diagnose and repair a specific bug as a single-stride substrate story. Reproduces, identifies root cause, writes failing test, applies minimal fix, creates story at stage:review. Triggers on 'fix bug X', 'fix the issue with Y'.

skills.name: epic-design
skills.description: Decompose an epic at stage:drafting into child features with declared depends_on chains. Reads the epic body, foundation docs, and codebase; identifies feature-level capability arcs; writes child feature files at .work/active/features/ at stage:drafting; advances epic stage to implementing. Design-family entry point for kind:epic — autopilot routes here. For project-level seeding from foundation docs use /agile-workflow:epicize.

skills.name: feature-design
skills.description: Design a feature at stage:drafting with no specialized tag. Greenfield design — produces detailed implementation units written INTO the feature's body, spawns child stories with declared depends_on, advances stage to implementing. For [refactor] features use /agile-workflow:refactor-design; for [perf] use /agile-workflow:perf-design; for epics use /agile-workflow:epic-design.

skills.name: refactor-design
skills.description: Plan an incremental, safe refactor for a feature with tags:[refactor] at stage:drafting. Code-smell scan via parallel agents, before/after step shape, risk + rollback per step. Plan written INTO feature body, child stories spawned, stage advances to implementing.

skills.name: perf-design
skills.description: Profile bottlenecks and design optimized solutions for a feature with tags:[perf] at stage:drafting. Strict optimization hierarchy (algorithmic > I/O > idioms > parallelism), benchmark scaffolds. Plan written INTO feature body, child stories spawned, stage advances to implementing.

skills.name: e2e-test-design
skills.description: Design comprehensive e2e tests with service-level mocks only (LocalStack, Testcontainers, WireMock, Toxiproxy) for a feature with tags:[e2e-test] or [testing] at stage:drafting. Family member alongside feature-design / refactor-design / perf-design — writes design INTO feature body, spawns child stories with depends_on, advances stage to implementing. Covers four test styles: golden-path, failure-mode, chaos, fuzzing. Two extra modes: --bootstrap seeds an e2e test epic + child features when none exists; --audit analyzes the test program's shape (mock-boundary violations, missing taxonomy layers, journey gaps, suite-level tautology) and produces items. Audit accepts --release <version> to plug into release-deploy as a quality gate.

skills.name: implement
skills.description: Write code from a substrate item at stage:implementing. Use for stories (no children to orchestrate) and for features without child stories. Reads design from item body, writes code, runs build+tests, advances stage implementing -> review. Detects 'land mode' when code already exists in the working tree (typical for retroactively-captured items) and validates+commits+advances rather than re-implementing. For features with child stories, /agile-workflow:implement-orchestrator is the default — it mirrors implement's per-story logic across waves.

skills.name: implement-orchestrator
skills.description: Default implementation path for features with child stories. Walks the depends_on graph, spawns Sonnet sub-agents in waves capped at 3 per wave (no upper limit on waves — bigger features just mean more waves). Each per-story prompt mirrors /agile-workflow:implement's workflow (land mode, dep check, design-flaw rollback). Single-story features run as a one-agent wave.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Review a substrate item at stage:review. Runs structured code review (correctness, tests, design, security, breaking changes, foundation-doc alignment), classifies findings as blockers/important/nits, triages findings into items, advances stage to done if approved or back to implementing if changes needed. Auto-advances parent (feature or epic) to review when its last child reaches done. Autonomous-safe — autopilot calls this directly to drain stage:review items without human intervention.

skills.name: release-deploy
skills.description: Cut a release: bind items to a version, run all configured gates in CONVENTIONS.md order (default: security -> tests -> cruft -> docs -> patterns), wait for readiness (all bound + gate-produced items at stage:done), ship per release mapping, archive items via git mv to releases/<version>/. Idempotent.

skills.name: gate-security
skills.description: Security gate over a release bundle. Discovers stack, audits the bundle's code changes against domain checklists, produces items in .work/active/ with gate_origin:security and tags:[security]. Item-producer, NOT a pass/fail report.

skills.name: gate-tests
skills.description: Test-quality gate over a release bundle. Derives expected coverage from each item's acceptance criteria (NOT from implementation code). Identifies gaps, tautological tests, and e2e seams between bound items. Produces items with gate_origin:tests.

skills.name: gate-cruft
skills.description: Cruft gate over a release bundle. Scans bundle changes for AI-accumulated debris (dead code, stale comments, compatibility shims, defensive bloat, over-abstraction) using language-aware tools and heuristics. Produces items with gate_origin:cruft.

skills.name: gate-docs
skills.description: Documentation gate. Enforces rolling-foundation principle: scans bound items for foundation-doc drift (assertions in docs/ that no longer match implementation), changelog gaps, repo-skill staleness, pattern-skill staleness. Produces items with gate_origin:docs.

skills.name: gate-patterns
skills.description: Patterns gate. Scans bundle changes for reusable code structures (3+ occurrences). Writes pattern skills to .claude/skills/patterns/, regenerates .claude/rules/patterns.md index, produces a tracking item with gate_origin:patterns.

skills.name: bold-refactor
skills.description: Find beautiful code abstractions and cross-cutting simplifications via conceptual lenses (elimination, unification, inversion, algebraic, declarative, domain crystallization). Produces a refactor EPIC with child features tagged [refactor] and depends_on chains. User-invocable only — too aggressive for auto-trigger.

skills.name: autopilot
skills.description: Drain a substrate queue autonomously. Picks the next ready item respecting depends_on, invokes the right skill (epic-design for kind:epic at drafting; feature-design / refactor-design / perf-design for kind:feature at drafting; implement / implement-orchestrator for implementing; review for review), advances stage, repeats. Epic-scoped by default; --all drains all of .work/active/; free-text scope arg interpreted by the agent. Only .work/backlog/ is out of scope. Circuit-breaker halts on items that bounce review→implementing more than twice. Watchdog /loop tasks survive compaction.

skills.name: research
skills.description: Research external libraries, APIs, SDKs, and patterns. Evaluates options against project needs, produces a research document and an auto-loading reference skill.

skills.name: repo-eval
skills.description: Multi-dimensional codebase evaluation with verified scoring. Launches parallel explore agents, cross-checks findings, produces calibrated 1-10 scorecard across architecture, code quality, testing, documentation, CI/CD, error handling, security, DX, and maintainability with prioritized recommendations.

skills.name: tool-evaluator
skills.description: Structured self-evaluation of agent tool usage in the current conversation. Analyzes confusion points, inefficiencies, missing capabilities, and API surface friction. Produces a prioritized .md report with actionable recommendations for tool authors.

skills.name: refactor-conventions-creator
skills.description: Create or update a project-specific refactor-conventions skill. Explores the repo, researches stack-specific best practices, interviews about both stylistic and structural preferences, then generates a combined skill with per-rule reference files.

plugins.name: ux-ui-design
plugins.description: HTML/CSS/JS mockup-first UI/UX design. Generate multi-option screen mockups, multi-page user flows, and design-system palettes as throwaway single-file mockups in .mockups/ for alignment before implementation. Loosely integrates with agile-workflow.

skills.name: ux-ui-principles
skills.description: Reference for the mockup-first UI/UX design convention. Auto-loads when designing UI surfaces or when screens/flows/palette skills run. Carries the .mockups/ storage layout, the REQUIRED/OPTIONAL/SKIP decision matrix, the linking convention to agile-workflow items, and the single-file HTML/CSS/JS tech rule. Also installs the rule into CLAUDE.md on first invocation.

skills.name: screens
skills.description: Generate N distinct single-screen HTML mockup options (default 4) for one UI surface in .mockups/screens/<feature-id>/, open them side-by-side via an index.html grid, and ask the user to pick or describe a hybrid. Iterates with feedback until sign-off.

skills.name: flows
skills.description: Generate a multi-page user-flow mockup in .mockups/flows/<flow-name>/ with chrome matched to the flow's topology — sequential (prev/next), hub-and-spoke (persistent nav bar), or hybrid (sequence + cross-jump breadcrumb). Renders both navigation patterns when both fit; renders the one that fits when only one does. Always includes an index.html navigator that visualizes the topology. For signup, checkout, onboarding, settings, dashboards, and other multi-screen journeys that need sign-off before implementation.

skills.name: palette
skills.description: Generate the project's design-system mockup — color palette, typography scale, and a reusable tokens.css — as HTML preview pages in .mockups/design-system/. Produces multiple palette + type options for the user to pick, then locks the chosen tokens. Run before components and screens/flows so all mocks share a coherent visual language.

skills.name: components
skills.description: Generate the project's component library — buttons, form fields, cards, modals, toasts, dropdowns, plus project-specific unique components — as a showcase page (.mockups/design-system/components.html) and a reusable .mockups/design-system/components.css that screens and flows link. Runs AFTER palette (depends on tokens.css) and BEFORE screens/flows to give every mock identical primitives and a unified look.

skills.name: adopt
skills.description: Bootstrap mockup-first design onto an existing project. Scans the codebase to inventory every UI surface and design-system fragment, audits for inconsistencies (component duplication, hardcoded colors, accessibility gaps, layout drift, copy drift, missing empty/error states), then orchestrates palette → components → screens → flows in MIRROR mode (faithful capture with remediation proposals) or REIMAGINE mode (redesign informed by the audit). Produces .mockups/adoption-report.md as the inventory + findings + decisions artifact. Use when adopting the plugin onto a project that already has UI code, auditing UI quality, or re-syncing mocks with code that has drifted.
